title: Personal Data Protection (Fees) Regulations 2013
issuedBy: Minister charged with responsibility for Personal Data Protection
summary: These Regulations set out the fees payable for registration of data users, renewal of registration, replacement of certificates, data subject access requests, and other matters under the Personal Data Protection Act 2010. All fees collected are credited to the Personal Data Protection Fund established under Part V of the Act.

provisions.title: Citation and Commencement
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Personal Data Protection (Fees) Regulations 2013 and shall come into force on 15 November 2013.

provisions.title: Registration Fees
provisions.content: The fee payable for an application for registration as a data user is as specified in the First Schedule to these Regulations. Fees are differentiated by the class of data user and the size of the data user's operations, as determined by the number of data subjects whose data is processed or the annual revenue of the data user, whichever metric produces the higher fee category.
requirements: Fees must be paid at the time of submitting the application — applications without the correct fee will not be processed
Fees are non-refundable once the application has been assessed
Large data users (more than 100,000 data subjects) pay a higher registration fee

smallDataUser.description: Data users processing personal data of fewer than 10,000 data subjects

mediumDataUser.description: Data users processing personal data of 10,000 to 100,000 data subjects

largeDataUser.description: Data users processing personal data of more than 100,000 data subjects

multipleClasses.description: Additional fee per additional class of data user

provisions.title: Renewal Fees
provisions.content: The fee payable for renewal of registration is the same as the initial registration fee applicable at the time of renewal. Where the data user's size category has changed since the previous registration, the fee applicable to the new category applies.
requirements: Renewal fees must accompany the renewal application
Fee category is assessed at the time of the renewal application based on current data subject numbers

provisions.title: Replacement Certificate Fee
provisions.content: The fee payable for the replacement of a lost, stolen, or defaced certificate of registration is RM100 per certificate. The data user must submit a statutory declaration attesting to the loss or defacement before a replacement will be issued.
requirements: Statutory declaration required for lost or stolen certificates
Replacement certificate has the same expiry date as the original

provisions.title: Data Access Request Fee
provisions.content: A data user may charge a fee for complying with a data access request under section 30 of the Act. The maximum fee is RM10 per access request, regardless of the quantity of data disclosed. No fee may be charged for correcting inaccurate data or for acknowledging receipt of an access request. Where a request is refused, no fee may be retained.
requirements: The fee must be notified to the data subject before it is charged
If the data user charges a fee that exceeds RM10, the excess must be refunded
Fee waiver is recommended for data subjects demonstrating financial hardship
Where a request is refused, any fee paid must be refunded

provisions.title: Complaint Registration Fee
provisions.content: The fee payable for lodging a complaint with the Commissioner under Part VIII of the Act is nil. No fee is charged for complaints lodged by data subjects or any person on their behalf. This ensures that the complaint mechanism remains accessible to all data subjects regardless of their financial circumstances.
requirements: No fee is payable for data subject complaints
Complaints from third parties (e.g., advocacy organizations) on behalf of data subjects are also fee-free

provisions.title: Appeal Tribunal Filing Fee
provisions.content: The fee payable for filing an appeal to the Personal Data Protection Appeal Tribunal under Part VII of the Act is RM500 for each appeal. The Tribunal may waive or reduce the fee in cases of financial hardship or where the public interest in the appeal is significant.
requirements: Appeal fee must be paid at the time of lodging the notice of appeal
The Tribunal may order that the fee be refunded where the appeal is successful
Applications for fee waiver must be made to the Registrar of the Tribunal

provisions.title: Payment Methods
provisions.content: All fees payable under these Regulations may be paid by electronic transfer to the account designated by the Commissioner, by bank draft made payable to 'Jabatan Perlindungan Data Peribadi', or by such other methods as the Commissioner may publish in the Gazette from time to time. Cash payments are accepted only at the Commissioner's office during business hours.
requirements: Electronic payment is the preferred method
Payment receipts must be retained and submitted with the relevant application
Cheques are not accepted from data users outside Malaysia
